paper_title: Comparison of simulation and proxy data

methods.description: This method involves using climate simulation data to understand atmospheric circulation changes and precipitation seasonality over a study area during specific historical periods (HE1 and YD). The simulation data were generated using the LOVECLIM model, which incorporates various climate forcings and freshwater forcing to mimic changes in the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC).

steps.description: We use the results of a climate simulation of Weldeab et al. (2016) to better understand the atmospheric circulation changes and seasonality of precipitation increase over the study area during HE1 and the YD.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve climate simulation data from Weldeab et al. (2016)
inputs: climate simulation data from Weldeab et al. (2016)
outputs: understanding of atmospheric circulation changes and precipitation seasonality

steps.description: The transient simulation data were generated using LOVECLIM, a fully coupled climate model of intermediate complexity.
steps.searchable_summary: generate transient simulation data using LOVECLIM model

steps.description: Freshwater forcing is applied to the North Atlantic region 50°N-70°N to mimic AMOC changes during the HE1-Bølling-Allerød (BA)-YD transition.
steps.searchable_summary: apply freshwater forcing to North Atlantic region in simulation

steps.description: In response to the freshwater forcings, the simulated AMOC decreased significantly and, as a consequence, the heat export to the North Atlantic was reduced and North Atlantic sea surface water cooled substantially.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze AMOC response and heat export reduction in simulation

steps.description: The latter triggered large-scale atmospheric circulation changes that led to a southward shift and intensification of austral summer precipitation over the Indian Ocean domain, including over the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize atmospheric circulation changes and precipitation shifts
